Title: The Theory Of Everything, Pt. 2
Comment:
I don't know what you mean, not very progressive. This is a certified prog artist and a sub-2 minute track, that isn't just album opening or interstitial atmospherics, but a legit song.

It also helps that, in my opinion, the 'Theory of Everything' riff may be the best musical motif that Arjen has ever written! 

I don't expect this to do well, but I've made myself happy, so anything else is a bonus.

Title: David Fucking Bowie
Comment: Bowie was my first thought as one of the few things this country has produced that gives me any sort of national pride. But turns out Bowie doesn't tend to sing about how cool David Bowie is, so instead you get one of my favourite Powerman tracks, which I do think has enough of a reverential national anthem vibe to fit the bill. Also it's fun to imagine a group of children singing in a school assembly.

Title: The Planets, Op. 32: IV. Jupiter, the Bringer of Jollity
Artist(s): Gustav Holst, Berliner Philharmoniker, Herbert von Karajan
Comment:
Was struggling for inspiration for this one, so here's an insight of how I got here: "Maybe we should go back to British folk music, what is that like? Oh, the British folk revival influenced Gustav Holst? Oh, he was British?"

Our anthem is a bit of a dirge, so maybe we bring some jollity to it; take the section from ~01:35-02:20 and write some lyrics to fit (I'm not brimming with enough national pride to be best placed for this, so I'm not gonna try).
The theme from around 03:00 could also work, but lets not just re-do Land of Hope and Glory ...

And if this all seems too sincere, just make the lyrics about willies and bums or muddy monarchs!

Title: I Predict A Riot
Comment:
✅ Clear and simple lyrics to sing along to - You already know them!
✅ Paints a more realistic view of the nation than God Save the X/Rule Britannia
✅ Well-known British band - But not London-centric.
✅ References popular British past times - Binge drinking / getting lairy.
✅ Highlights historic figure John Smeaton, “The father of civil engineering”.
✅ Somewhat more jaunty than the drivel we have now.
✅ Highly recognisable tune.
✅ Zero brown nosing of old sausage fingers Charlie - Doesn't even get a mention in.

Title: The Land Of Do What You’re Told
Comment: I was going to submit an absolute Bellamy Banger but then I thought it was a bit rude to submit a 6 minute unaccompanied ballad from Elmer P Bleaty

Title: Kernkraft 400 - DJ Gius Mix, Radio Edit
Comment:
UK/England anthem submission to replace God Save the King. 

When do anthems matter the most? Sporting events! What's the worst thing about them? People not knowing the lyrics. Kernkraft 400 solves for both.

Trim off the first 27 seconds. Get the crowd chanting the riff. The true patriots can shout the Zombie Nations.

A powerful representation of modern values that supports UK PLC into a new digital millennium.
